div1.entryFree:
Dim, vb. 1) to impair the sight of, to hinder from seeing, either by dazzling or clouding the eyes: her eyes are by his flaming torch --ed and controlled, Lucr. 448. some sudden qualm hath struck me at the heart and --ed mine eyes, H6B I, 1, 55. I, 2, 6. III, 1, 218. H6C V, 2, 16.
2) to make invisible or less visible, to darken, to tarnish: often is his (the sun's) gold complexion --ed, Sonn. 18, 6. R2 III, 3, 66. R3 II, 2, 102. IV, 4, 16. Tit. IV, 4, 82. Figuratively: let not sloth d. your honours new-begot, H6A I, 1, 79. H6B I, 1, 125. And == to eclipse: so doth the greater glory d. the less, Merch. V, 93.
